note: Reduce RunPod Pod image size by moving ComfyUI (source + ~5GB deps) to the network volume. Spike-gated plan; investigation complete (research/investigation.md).

summary: LIVE CONTRAST (MPI-187 cu130 test): RunPod official runpod/comfyui:cuda13.0 boots INSTANT vs our ~5min. Two causes, both actionable: (1) IMAGE CACHE — their image is RunPods own first-party image, warm-cached across their host fleet, zero registry pull; ours is a cold GHCR pull on every fresh host. (2) NO-VOLUME BOOT — they bake ComfyUI to /opt/comfyui-baked + cp -r to local overlay disk on boot (seconds, no network); our start.sh streams ComfyUI+deps from the 1GB/s network volume. NOTE this REFRAMES this cards fix: moving ComfyUI TO the volume (this cards current plan) is the OPPOSITE of what makes theirs fast — theirs wins by baking INTO the image + warm fleet-cache + local-disk copy. Re-evaluate: the real lever may be (a) shrink the image so it pulls/caches faster, (b) register a RunPod TEMPLATE so RunPod fleet-caches our image, (c) bake+cp-r from a local image layer instead of volume-stream. HOSTING Q (user): can we get our image warm on RunPod like theirs? Yes-ish — RunPod pre-caches template images across hosts; smaller image caches on more hosts; Docker Hub / RunPod infra pulls faster than GHCR. Combo = smaller image (this card) + template registration ~= their instant boot. Cross-ref MPI-187.

Approach-note added: MPI-187 cu130 finding supersedes/re-scopes this card's original approach. See top-of-description ⚠️ banner. move-to-volume in tension with RunPod bake-and-cache; re-scope spike, ride the MPI-189 rebuild.

RE-SCOPED + retitled + priority->high. Premise inverted by MPI-187: not image-too-big but cold-GHCR-pull + volume-streaming. New direction = HYBRID bake-and-cache (RunPod-style: bake ComfyUI in image + cp -r to local disk + fleet-cache via template) + SHRINK, NOT volume-relocation (which fights the slow path + needs risky aimdo .so relocation). Card now DECIDES image architecture via a cheap decision spike (does RunPod fleet-cache a custom template image?) and GATES MPI-189. Sequence moved: 186 (decide) BEFORE 189 (build cu130 that way).

Begin implementation. Plan rewritten to the rescoped decision-spike + shrink-survey (verified live Dockerfile/start.sh: ComfyUI already boots from local /opt, NOT a volume stream, so the old volume-relocation plan is abandoned). Card now = decision doc + spike evidence that gates MPI-189.

plan.md/checklist.md/validation.md/files.json rewritten for the rescope. New shape: Phase 1 decision spike (does a custom template image fleet-cache? template vs bare imageName? GHCR vs Docker Hub cold-pull) + Phase 2 shrink survey (multi-stage devel->runtime for sage, cache/pyc strip) + Phase 3 write research/decision.md that MPI-189 builds to. Verify mode user-ux (all live Pod ops USER-run). investigation.md kept as reference evidence for why NOT to relocate to the volume.

Spike sharpened by the RunPod template screenshot: the instant image was RunPods OWN Official image (runpod/comfyui:cuda13.0), not community/verified. Reframed the spike to ONE cheap A/B test = is the Official tier fleet-pre-seeded (Cause A, custom images cant match) or is instant just registry-proximity + host-reuse (Cause B, chase-able)? Deploy Official cold + a popular Community image cold + our image cold-vs-warm on fresh hosts. KILLED the template-registration lever (a template is deploy-config, not a byte cache; our create path passes imageName directly). User deferred the goal (parity vs bearable) to the A/B result; parked a REBASE-on-official fork to evaluate ONLY if Cause A. Registry (GHCR vs Docker Hub) + multi-stage -runtime shrink stay as Phase 2.

RESEARCH-SETTLED + direction locked (user-confirmed). Docs research (research/runpod-coldstart-docs.md) settled the cold-start mechanism from docs, no Pods spent: per-host layer cache; our private GHCR image cold-pulls every fresh host; Official is popularity+label, not obtainable. DEAD levers killed: template registration, Official-chasing, FlashBoot (Serverless-only). Serverless assessed (research/serverless-fit.md) + DROPPED — breaks live-preview streaming + warm session; only ever a future additive batch-export feature. User confirmed: already on Secure Cloud (not a lever); Stop-not-Delete already in app (rarely lands same host, bonus only). LOCKED DIRECTION = (1) GHCR -> Docker Hub, (2) shrink the baked image. Card reduced from discovery-spike to: measure GHCR-vs-Hub pull + docker-history size table, produce shrink cut-list, write research/decision.md that MPI-189 builds to.

SHRINK MEASURED (docker history, local images, no Pods). Current cu128=50.2GB / cu124=42.2GB. ~13GB removable without losing capability: (1) 6.94GB = the runpod base's NIGHTLY torch we pip-uninstall then replace = pure waste; (2) ~6GB = CUDA -dev toolkit (cudart-dev/cublas-dev/nsight/nvprof) needed only to BUILD sage -> multi-stage: build sage on -devel, ship on -runtime base = biggest clean cut. cu124 also carries a 6.11GB /opt/conda (pytorch conda base) dead weight. KEEP: pinned torch trio (7GB, arch-bound) + sage binary + nodes(4.26GB) + ComfyUI(1.19GB) + node weights(537MB). Target ~50->37GB on cu130 before Docker Hub gain. Wrote research/decision.md = the spec MPI-189 builds to (cu130 -runtime base + multi-stage sage + Docker Hub push + app repoint of POD_IMAGE_BASE). ONLY live op left = USER measures GHCR-vs-DockerHub cold-pull seconds.

DECISION CARD CONCLUDED (user-approved). Deliverable = research/decision.md, the spec MPI-189 builds to. Settled from docs + local docker history, zero Pods spent. Decision: (1) GHCR->Docker Hub, (2) single cu130 -runtime base + multi-stage sage build (~30% smaller compressed pull ~18->12GB), (3) placement already settled, serverless OUT. Size discrepancy reconciled: 50GB uncompressed on-disk vs ~18-20GB compressed pull (prior agents 'under 20GB' = the pull; both correct). GHCR-vs-DockerHub pull measurement folded into 189 (first cu130 deploy = real before/after). MPI-189 card body updated + ungated.
Archived completed card during board cleanup.
